checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: Operating cash flow data for 2011-04-01 to 2011-09-30 (Q2/Q3 2011) not provided, limiting assessment of divergence during the period of the large Q3 2011 net loss
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2011 cash flow statement (last available OCF data point)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=234020657.82 (2010 annual) on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=284705944.40 (2010 annual), ~82.2% margin; GrossProfit=63619294.93 (2011-07-01 to 2011-09-30) on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=79106647.73 (2011-07-01 to 2011-09-30), ~80.4% margin
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010 income statement; 10-Q Q3 2011 income statement

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-16388077.96 (2008 annual); NetIncomeLoss=3457641.56 (2009 annual); NetIncomeLoss=46604936.17 (2010 annual)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Multiple SEC correspondence/UPLOAD filings: CORRESP 2010-08-11, UPLOAD 2010-08-30, CORRESP 2010-09-03, UPLOAD 2010-09-28, CORRESP 2010-10-12, UPLOAD 2010-10-28

2010-12-31 balance sheet figures (Assets=477467079.88, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=45620865.66, LiabilitiesCurrent=64876023.81) are sourced from accession 0001193125-11-297771 (10-Q filed 2011-11-04) rather than the original FY2010 10-K accession 0000950123-11-018857

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities data available through 2011-01-01 to 2011-03-31 only; no Q2/Q3 2011 operating cash flow figures provided
No segment, footnote, or MD&A narrative text provided to explain the FY2011 asset/liability swings or the Q3 2011 net loss

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of bad debt allowance/reserve as a percentage of receivables (from ~7.1% to ~3.4% of gross AR) despite growing receivables and revenue, which would reduce bad debt expense and inflate reported net income in FY2010, the same year net income jumped roughly 13x versus FY2009.

Unexplained large swings in total assets and current liabilities around a large equity raise (424B5) and a subsequent ~$40M net loss quarter suggest possible acquisition-related purchase accounting, contingent consideration remeasurement, or impairment/write-off charges that were not smoothly reflected in prior quarters, raising risk that prior-period assets/goodwill were carried at values requiring later correction.

FY2010 year-end balance sheet figures reported in the Q3 2011 10-Q (filed 2011-11-04) differ in source accession from the original FY2010 10-K filing, suggesting a reclassification or restatement of prior-period comparative amounts that was not clearly disclosed as such in the provided data.

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts declined from ~7.1% to ~3.4% of accounts receivable (2009 to 2010) despite AR and revenue growth, coinciding with a 13x jump in net income in FY2010 (a classic reserve-release earnings-boost pattern)

Extreme balance-sheet volatility in 2011 (Assets swinging from ~$480M to ~$758M to ~$664M across three quarters, current liabilities nearly doubling in Q3) culminating in a sudden ~$40.2M net loss in Q3 2011 with no corroborating operating cash flow data available

FY2010 comparative balance sheet figures reported in the Q3 2011 10-Q differ in source accession from the original FY2010 10-K, and a 10-K/A amendment was filed roughly three months after the original FY2009 10-K, alongside multiple SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD filings in 2010
